title: calystegia collina subsp. oxyphylla
intro: Calystegia collina subsp. oxyphylla is a subspecies of the bindweed plant, a perennial vine native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calystegia collina subsp. oxyphylla include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Calystegia collina subsp. oxyphylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedy.
